paragraphs.text: Student of the Jean Monnet Modules project “DERECHO”: ‘Defense of European Rights and Excellence for Consumers HHarmonisation’, No 101239437. Project financed by the European Union. Jean Monnet Actions in the field of Higher Education: Modules. Call for tenders ERASMUS-JMO-2025-HEI-TCH-RSCH. The EU’s policies on harm and consumption seem to me to be really interesting, but I believe that they could be better done. Firstly, national rules stemming from those imposed by the EU are often not implemented and many companies circumvent the law. In this respect, and in existing legislation, the focus should therefore be on whether it is actually carried out. On the other hand, it seems to me important to look forward to legislating without any kind of fear. As looking for consumer protection we could end up restricting basic freedoms within trade and the market; we are lagging behind the US and China. It is important for the European Commission to focus its forces as much as possible on reducing taxes on economic traffic, attracting non-EU industries and companies, defending the national economy and, in short, focusing forces to the greatest possible extent on becoming the continent rey. The policy of harm and consumption does not say that it is not important, but importance must be sought. Global hegemony, defending the national against the global, our common values, promoting the primary and secondary sector in Europe and not restricting so many freedoms.
